note: resolved by SHAPE, not by name: this module spells it 'aÑo' in 2025, whose real bytes are b'a\xc3\x83\xe2\x80\x98o' — 'año' double-encoded mojibake. harmonize.find_year_col() matches a...o and then validates the values are plausible years.
JOIN KEY 1/4 — normalized before any join
JOIN KEY 4/4. Module 85 is answered by ONE selected person aged 18+ per household, not by everyone — it is person-level but NOT the full roster.

THE WEIGHT, AND IT IS RENAMED FOUR TIMES. From INEI's own labels (perudata.dictionary.weights('85')): factor07 2004-2009, facgob07 2010-2011, factor07 again 2012-2013 (relabelled 'factor ajustado'), facgob07 2014, famiegob07 2015-2025. NO fixed name finds it — resolve with harmonize.find_weight_cols(). It expands to the ADULT population (25.2M in 2025), not to households or to everyone, because only one 18+ respondent per household answers this module.

2020 ONLY. In the COVID year ENAHO was partly conducted BY TELEPHONE, and INEI shipped a SECOND weight for the in-person (presencial) subsample. Every module has a 2020-only 'factor_p' twin for the same reason. Choosing between them changes who your sample represents — famiegob07 is the annual weight, facgob_p covers only the presencial cases (n=15,721 of 32,447 in 2020).

'en el Peru, la democracia funciona'. The governance questionnaire is REDESIGNED repeatedly (26 columns in 2004, 116 in 2014, 244 in 2025), so a variable name here is far less stable than in the core modules. Check the label per year with dictionary.variable() before pooling.
